notes.title: Customize Order of Ticket Detail Sections
notes.bodyHtml:
Application administrators can choose the order that the sections of the ticket detail page will display in, allowing you to put the ticket description or custom attributes above the ticket's standard attributes section. This can be configured in TDAdmin > Applications > [Ticketing Application] > Settings, in the Detail Page Order property.

This feature was previously released in a maintenance release of version 12.1.

notes.title: Client Portal Interface Updates
notes.bodyHtml:
The TeamDynamix client portal interface has been updated to use concepts from the technician user interface for a more modern look and feel. The following changes are included:

  • Tables in the client portal use the format and style used by the technician interface.
  • Action buttons (e.g. Request Service, Edit Article) have a new format to allow more actions in the same screen space, allowing more requestable items to be seen at once.
  • Article and Service detail pages use more of the screen width.
  • Category tiles, project tiles, and HTML modules have updated borders and styles.
  • Certain actions that opened as pop-up windows now open with in the page.

Day 1 Impact - These changes take place automatically for all organizations with the launch of version 12.2.

notes.title: Budget and Capacity Planner
notes.bodyHtml:
A new web-based version of the Budget and Capacity Planner is available, accessible from the Portfolio Planning standard reports page and the Downloads app. The Budget and Capacity Planner allows project and resource managers to view the resource and budget allocations of projects and requests and evaluate how shifting those allocations would affect resources' capacity.

To learn more about using the Budget and Capacity Planner, see Getting Started with Budget and Capacity Planner.

notes.title: Project Applications
notes.bodyHtml:
Organizations can define multiple project applications, with separate visibility, security roles, configurations, and administration. Areas of the system in the Projects, Project Templates, and Portfolio Planning standard applications will now be administered inside the segmented Projects application.

All project applications will share configurations and information related to resources and their schedules, so that resource planning can happen without overlapping requests from separate applications.

For more information about Project Applications, see these articles:

  • Project Applications Overview
  • Moving Projects
  • Moving Project Requests
Day 1 Impact - The changes to move projects into an application structure take place automatically. Users will see the following impacts:
  • Project and Project Request-related pages within TDAdmin have been moved into the Project Application.
  • Security Roles will be generated within Project Applications that provide users with the same access as they have in version 12.1.
  • The Briefcase, Document Workflows, and Project Templates pages have been removed from the View Applications menu. Users with access to these applications will see them in the Settings menu of the Project application.
  • Feed entries for projects will be updated asynchronously during the upgrade, meaning that during the upgrade weekend, feed entries may not appear on projects and their component items until that feed entry has been updated. The most recent feed entries will be updated first.

notes.title: Automatic Ticket Assignment
notes.bodyHtml:
Automatic Ticket Assignment allows organizations to configure round robin-style ticket assignment for their Groups. At the group level, administrators can define rotation order, the source of tickets that the setting should apply to, as well as settings for any group members that should be excluded from automatic ticket assignment.

To learn more about using Automatic Ticket Assignment, see Getting Started with Automatic Ticket Assignment.

notes.title: Technician and Admin Interface Accessibility
notes.bodyHtml:
The technician and administrator interfaces are now more accessible, with changes to meet current accessibility standards. All areas of TDNext, TDWorkManagement and TDAdmin now conform with the WCAG 2.1 AA standard.

As part of this change, help text is displayed throughout the system to explain date formats for date pickers and to identify required fields

Day 1 Impact - The changes to help text are applied automatically.

notes.title: Warning Message for Non-Production Environments
notes.bodyHtml:
A new warning message appears in non-production environments at the top of the page. When enabled, this can be dismissed for an individual page but will reappear on the next page load. This feature can be enabled or disabled in TDAdmin > Organization Settings > Site Settings > Non-Production Environments.

Day 1 Impact - This setting will be enabled in all environments when upgraded to version 12.2, but will not show anything in Production.

notes.title: Configurable Session Timeout
notes.bodyHtml: Organization administrators can modify the session timeout in TDAdmin > Organization Settings > Site Settings. This controls how long a session remains active after a user becomes idle, and defaults 480 minutes (8 hours), which is the same timeout as applies to all organizations in version 12.1.

notes.title: Webhook Event Filtering
notes.bodyHtml:
When creating or editing a webhook, administrators can configure filtering that applies before the webhook is sent out from TeamDynamix. These filters apply to Created and Changed webhooks. Administrators will be prompted to include a filter whenever they create or edit a webhook with no filters, but webhook filters are not required.

To learn more about getting started with webhooks, see this article.

notes.title: User Option to Open New Pages in Tabs
notes.bodyHtml: A new setting allows users to choose how pages that currently open as side panels should open. The setting is available at My Profile > Settings > Edit.
notes.bodyText: A new setting allows users to choose how pages that currently open as side panels should open. The setting is available at My Profile > Settings > Edit.

notes.title: Attachment Count in Reports
notes.bodyHtml: A new Attachment Count field is available in ticket and knowledge base article report builder reports.
notes.bodyText: A new Attachment Count field is available in ticket and knowledge base article report builder reports.

notes.title: Dynamic Widgets in Analysis Dashboard
notes.bodyHtml: Dynamic Widgets are now supported in the Analysis Dashboard. As with report builder reports, these will respect the user's "View All Application Instances in Analysis" permission to determine which items from segmented applications are available.
notes.bodyText: Dynamic Widgets are now supported in the Analysis Dashboard. As with report builder reports, these will respect the user's "View All Application Instances in Analysis" permission to determine which items from segmented applications are available.

notes.title: Customize Report Column Names
notes.bodyHtml: When building a report, users can customize the Display Name for columns using the new Display Name field.
notes.bodyText: When building a report, users can customize the Display Name for columns using the new Display Name field.

notes.title: Shared Dashboards
notes.bodyHtml:
Work Management users can share dashboards with each other directly from the dashboard's settings page, with the choice to share with specific users or with groups. Sharing a dashboard requires a new "Share Dashboards" permission which will not be automatically added to any security roles, but once the permission is granted, administrators no longer need to help configure and share dashboards with users.

To learn more about Shared Dashboards, see this article.

notes.title: Friendly Client Portal URLs
notes.bodyHtml:
URLs for end user facing pages in the client portal have been updated to be descriptive of the page that they link to. Existing links to these pages will automatically redirect to the new page URL, and renaming a page will support old versions of the descriptive URL.

The following pages will be supported:

  • Knowledge Base Category
  • Knowledge Base Article Detail
  • Service Category
  • Service Detail
  • Service Form
  • Service Offering Detail
  • Service Offering Form
  • Pages (client portal pages defined in TDAdmin).
    • The Page URL will be configurable in TDAdmin > Applications > Client Portal Application > Pages. This will be set automatically based on the page's name for existing pages.

Example URL change for this article:

  • Old URL: https://solutions.teamdynamix.com/TDClient/1965/Portal/KB/ArticleDet?ID=170730
  • New URL: https://solutions.teamdynamix.com/TDClient/1965/Portal/KB/Article/170730/12-2-Release-Notes
Day 1 Impact - This change will apply automatically to all client portals. Any existing link to the affected pages will permanently redirect to the new URL, so you do not need to update links that exist in user-generated content in TeamDynamix or in other systems.

notes.title: Minor Enhancements included in 4.0.2
notes.bodyHtml:
ID Component Problem Solution
29912949 CAI - Conversation The ticket response step allows users to wait for a response, but there is no place to include a question or prompt. This means the bot will present the ticket and then silently wait for the user to type something with no guidance. A say/ask capability has been added to the ticket response step, allowing bot builders to include a question or prompt when waiting for user input.
27850496 CAI - Live Chat When an end user requested a live agent chat, they had no visibility into how many people were ahead of them in the queue or their position as it changed. This lack of transparency could lead to frustration and cause users to abandon the chat. Live chat requestors are now presented with a "People Ahead of You" value, giving them real-time visibility into their queue position.
29300554 CAI - TDX Integration The TD WM Integration fields on the Environment dialog and Organization Settings tab did not provide any guidance about when a customer should configure these settings. A tooltip has been added to the TD WM Integration fields on the Environment dialog and Organization Settings to provide configuration guidance.
29642703 iPaaS - Auth. Methods / Credentials The help text for Google OAuth2 Server Authentication's "Subject" field incorrectly stated that the value should be the same as the ClientEmail. When using the Google Admin Directory Connector, the Subject should instead be an account with admin permissions that can be impersonated, not the service account. The help text for the "Subject" field in Google OAuth2 Server Authentication has been updated to accurately reflect the correct usage.
28880185 iPaaS - Connectors There was no native integration with key ITOM monitoring platforms such as Datadog, Dynatrace, Splunk, and New Relic. New connectors have been added for Datadog, Dynatrace, Splunk, and New Relic, enabling native integration with key ITOM monitoring platforms.
28381891 iPaaS - Other Various minor UI inconsistencies and issues. Minor UI tweaks and bug fixes have been applied.
29578191 Ticket Assist Agent - Dashboards / Metrics The statistics and logging for Assist Agent did not account for the new KB article generation capability, meaning its usage and associated resources were not being tracked. Assist Agent statistics and logging have been updated to include KB article generation usage and resource tracking.
29342766 Ticket Assist Agent - Agent Tester There was no way to verify whether a user had permissions to use a Response Template from Work Management that had been ingested into Assist Agent. A permission check has been added for Response Templates sourced from Work Management, ensuring only authorized users can access them.

2. Bug Fixes

Bug Fixes for 4.0.2

ID Component Problem Solution
29908458 CAI - Conversation Clicking a citation link that navigated to a KB article caused the conversation to restart in the new window. Bug has been resolved.
29736953 CAI - Tasks The VSA Show Step for TDX Ticketsappears but the header image is a broken image link. Bug has been resolved.
29894374 iPaaS - Flows The "Is Date" expression was not appearing in the expression picker within a flow and was only visible in the flow toolbox. Bug has been resolved.
29897356 iPaaS - Flows When using the "sheet" step, the columns within the sheet step disappear when trying to add or insert data into it. Bug has been resolved.
29859512 iPaaS - Flows Sheet Data types in Flows were not handling column names correctly. Often two underscores were pre-fixing the actual column name. Bug has been resolved.
29898728 CAI - UI/UX Full Command Bar mode was not persisting in the web UI in some cases. Bug has been resolved.
29872433 Ticket Assist Agent - UI/UX The Copy to Clipboard functionality in Ticket Assist Agent was not working. Bug has been resolved.

3. Reminders

Upcoming Proxy Minimum Version

Beginning with the summer release of the AI & Automation Layer (version 4.1), a minimum proxy version will be enforced. When 4.1 is released, any proxy running a version below 4.0 will immediately become inoperable. Going forward, the minimum supported proxy version will follow a rolling "last major release" basis — when the system upgrades to 4.2, the minimum will become 4.1, and so on.

Install .NET 10 Before the Summer 2026 Release

The proxy currently requires .NET 8 (as of proxy version 4.0.2). However, Microsoft will retire .NET 8 in November 2026, ending all security updates. More immediately, proxy version 4.1 — included in the summer release — will require .NET 10. Without .NET 10 installed, your proxy will not upgrade to 4.1 and will become inoperable when the 4.2 release enforces the minimum version requirement.

Please download and install the following on any machines that utilize the TeamDynamix iPaaS Proxy.

  • Direct download: ASP.NET Core Runtime 10 Hosting Bundle
  • Direct download: .NET Desktop Runtime 10

What You Should Do Now

If your proxy is already on version 4.0 or higher: Install .NET 10 to ensure your proxy upgrades automatically when 4.1 is released this summer.

If your proxy is below version 4.0: First, confirm that at least .NET 8 is installed so the proxy can reach version 4.0. Then install .NET 10 to support the 4.1 upgrade. Alternatively, you may install .NET 10 now and force-upgrade your proxy to 4.1 on the release date.

notes.title: Minor Enhancements included in 4.0.1
notes.bodyHtml:
Component Title Description
CAI - Conversation Add Conversational Task Interactions to Conversation Memory Interactions that occur within tasks are now included in the VSA's conversation memory. Tasks can also now optionally be configured to exclude their interactions from memory.
Dynamic Forms Increase Character Limit on Form Text Boxes The character limit for form text boxes has been increased beyond the previous 99,999 character limit to accommodate longer content.
Dynamic Forms Cancel Flow Occurrence When a Bad File Is Attached on Form Submission When a form is submitted with a file that fails the signature check, the resulting flow occurrence is now cancelled with an error message identifying the issue and the file name. Form Inbox Data records are no longer created if the file upload fails.
Flows/Tasks Add Ability to Export OpenAPI for Direct URLs The OpenAPI export buttons on the Documentation screen now offer dropdown options to export non-direct URLs (default), direct URLs only, or both. Direct URL exports also include the response data model defined in the flow's end step.
Flows/Tasks Add Ability to Toggle Comments and Improve Comment Action Button Accessibility Comment action buttons have been moved to the left side of each comment in the flow designer. A toggle button has been added to collapse or expand individual comments, and Expand All / Collapse All options are now available in the view menu.

notes.title: Form Definition System Upgrade
notes.bodyHtml:
As part of ongoing platform improvements, form definitions are being upgraded to a newer internal version. This upgrade adds unique identifiers to all form components (fields, columns, rows, and groups), laying the groundwork for more robust element referencing in future releases. This is the first of a multi-phase upgrade process that will continue in upcoming versions.

Day One Impact: All forms will be automatically upgraded by the system upon the 4.0.1 release. This process is estimated to take only minutes, and there is no downtime. Customers can continue editing and filling out forms during the upgrade. Behavior-wise, there is no impact to how forms look or function for customers or end users.

Because the system is updating form definitions behind the scenes, customers may notice that some forms appear as recently updated in the Forms grid (when sorting by Last Updated Time), in the Audit Trail, and in the Refresh Managed Apps dialog even though no manual changes were made. This is expected behavior resulting from the system upgrade and does not reflect any changes to form content or functionality. Similar system-level upgrades may occur in future releases as part of continued platform enhancements.

notes.title: Bug Fixes for 4.0.1
notes.bodyHtml:
Component Title Description
CAI - Administration Cannot Clear VSA App Description from VSA Config Page After clearing the description in VSA Configuration and saving, the description would reappear on the next page load. Clearing the VSA description now saves correctly.
CAI - Administration VSA Configuration Data Retention Page Shows Duplicate Field The Data Retention tab in VSA Configuration displayed two instances of the "Task/Flow Input/Output Data" field with different default values. Only one instance is now shown.
CAI - Administration VSA Live Chat Schedule Exclusions "Select All" Checkbox Cut Off In the VSA Configuration Live Chat Schedule Exclusions table, the "Select All" checkbox and column header were cut off due to a minimized column width. The column is now properly sized and fully visible.
CAI - Channels Teams Channel Not Linking Properly for Knowledge Query Citations When presenting Knowledge Query citation results as action links in a Teams-connected bot, clicking a link sent the URL as a chat response rather than opening it in a browser. This has been resolved.
CAI - Generative AI Memory Not Working as Expected in Generate Step Conversation memory passed to Generate steps via template variables was not being populated, causing subsequent Generate steps to lack context from earlier in the conversation. Conversation memory is now passed correctly.
CAI - Flows/Tasks Opening Generate Configuration Dialog While Saving Causes Unrecoverable Error Opening the Generate step configuration dialog while a task was still saving resulted in an error dialog that required a full page refresh to dismiss. This has been resolved.
CAI - UI / UX Transcript Step Data Model Not Updated Until Page Refresh After changing the output type on a Transcript step, the data model was not updated until the page was refreshed, causing downstream steps to use the previous type. The data model now updates immediately.
Accessibility Dynamic Forms - Accessibility Issues Dynamic Forms had accessibility issues identified by WAVE evaluation tools, and certain elements such as ListBox and Label - X-Large were not being recognized correctly by screen readers. Accessibility improvements have been made to address these issues.
Connectors - General Copying a Connector Does Not Copy All Supported Auth Methods When copying a connector that had multiple supported authentication methods, only the first method was carried over to the copy. All supported authentication methods are now copied when duplicating a connector.
Dashboards Flow Runs Dashboard Control Shows "[Unknown]" for Valid Flow Statuses The Flow Runs dashboard control displayed "[Unknown]" for several valid flow statuses, including Cancelled, Cancel Pending, Cancelled From Flow, Waiting Lock, and Waiting System. These statuses now display with their correct descriptive names.
Dynamic Forms Switch/Checkbox Fields with Label Position Set to Top Places Label on Left Setting the label position to "Top" on a Switch or Checkbox element placed the label to the left of the element instead of above it. Label positioning now behaves correctly for all positions on these elements.
Dynamic Forms Table Condition Not Working Properly A condition configured to hide a field based on table row length was not evaluating correctly after a row was deleted, hiding the field when it should have remained visible. This has been resolved.
Dynamic Forms Checkbox Labels Aligning Right When label position was set to "Right" on any form element, wrapped label text was aligned to the right instead of the left. For all element types, labels set to "Right" are now left-aligned, whether the text wraps or not.
Dynamic Forms Cannot Scroll in Disabled Text Areas Text area fields that were set to disabled could not be scrolled, making it impossible to read content beyond the visible area. Disabled text areas can now be scrolled.
Dynamic Forms Disabled List Box Values Not Retained on Submission When a List Box element was disabled, its selected value was not included in the form submission, unlike other disabled element types. Disabled List Box values are now retained on submission.
Dynamic Forms Custom Elements De-format HTML/CSS on Return to Design Mode HTML/CSS code entered in a Custom element was condensed into a single unformatted block after navigating away and returning to design mode. Custom element code formatting is now preserved.
Dynamic Forms Attachment Field Re-renders with Duplicate Name Causing Submit to Fail Under specific conditions involving a column with an initialization condition and multiple attachment fields, the Submit button became unresponsive with no validation message. Form submission now behaves correctly in this configuration.
Dynamic Forms State Selector Default Value Inconsistency The default selected value for a State selector element was inconsistent—sometimes showing Alabama and sometimes Alaska—depending on how the element was added to the form. The default is now consistently the first alphabetical value.
Dynamic Forms Table Template Row Click Condition Bug If a Table Template Row had a Click Condition using a field inside the table as its activator, toggling that field in one row could incorrectly affect other rows. Click conditions now apply only to the targeted row.
Dynamic Forms Table Rename Not Updating Condition References Renaming a table in a Dynamic Form did not update references to that table in existing conditions or expressions, causing those references to break. Renaming a table now automatically updates all references throughout the form.
Dynamic Forms Drop Down List Elements Show Different Shade of Red for Required Indicator Required Drop Down List elements displayed a different shade of red on their required indicator compared to other required element types. The indicator color is now consistent across all element types.
Flows/Tasks Nested Table Rename Breaks Fill From References Renaming a nested table in a flow broke any "Fill From" references to that table in downstream connector step configurations. Fill From references are now updated correctly when a nested table is renamed.
Flows/Tasks Flow Info Property Name Inconsistency in Data Picker (Environment vs. EnvironmentName) The FlowInfo environment property appeared as "EnvironmentName" in the FlowInfo object but as "Environment" in the Data Picker, requiring manual entry to reference it correctly. The property name is now consistent in both locations.
Flows/Tasks Inconsistent Behavior When Calling Child Flows with Stop vs. Stop with Error When a child flow used a "Stop with Error" step that also returned data, the parent flow did not receive that data. Behavior also differed depending on whether the call step was inside a block. Child flow return data is now correctly passed to the parent in both scenarios.
Flows/Tasks Send Approval Dialog Recipient Text Box Is Cut Off In the Send Approval step, the recipient text box was too narrow to display the full email address or username of a selected user. This affected Email, User, and User Group recipient types. Recipient entries now display fully.
Flows/Tasks Date Comparison Expression Has Duplicate Day Option and Other Issues The Date Comparison expression had a duplicate "Day" option in the dropdown, an inconsistent dropdown width, and missing entries in the help text. Duplicate entries have been removed, the dropdown width is now consistent, and the help text is complete.
Flows/Tasks Changing Iterator Index on Auto-Fill Breaks List Population Renaming the iterator index variable on an auto-filled list caused the list to populate with null values, and refreshing the page reverted the name while the null issue persisted. This has been resolved.
Flows/Tasks Autofill Table Condition Issues with Index Variable and Invalid References Renaming the index variable on an auto-filled table did not update existing references in condition expressions, and invalid condition references were not flagged in the Validation Pane. Both issues have been resolved.
Flows/Tasks Server Error When Adding New Flows to Library Attempting to add certain newly created flows to a flow library resulted in a server error. Existing flows were unaffected. This has been resolved.
Flows/Tasks Boolean Data Type Property Can Break Flow When Set to "Now" with Timezone Setting a Boolean property's value to "Now" with a timezone selected caused the flow to fail to load after saving. This has been resolved.
Flows/Tasks Export Dialog Shows "Export undefined Definition" from Home Activity Using the Export action for a flow from the Home Activity displayed an incorrect dialog header. The dialog now displays the correct title.
Libraries Missing Data When Replacing an Existing Library Flow When replacing an existing library item, the category and icon from the existing item were not pre-populated in the replacement dialog. These values now carry over when an existing item is selected for replacement.
UI/UX Dark Mode Not Implemented for Add Supported Auth Method Icon on Connectors The "+" icon for adding an additional supported authentication method on a connector did not display correctly in dark mode. The icon now respects dark mode styling.

4. Reminders

Upcoming Proxy Minimum Version

Beginning with the summer release of the AI & Automation Layer (version 4.1), a minimum proxy version will be enforced. When 4.1 is released, any proxy running a version below 4.0 will immediately become inoperable. Going forward, the minimum supported proxy version will follow a rolling "last major release" basis — when the system upgrades to 4.2, the minimum will become 4.1, and so on.

Install .NET 10 Before the Summer 2026 Release

The proxy currently requires .NET 8 (as of proxy version 4.0.1). However, Microsoft will retire .NET 8 in November 2026, ending all security updates. More immediately, proxy version 4.1 — included in the summer release — will require .NET 10. Without .NET 10 installed, your proxy will not upgrade to 4.1 and will become inoperable when the 4.2 release enforces the minimum version requirement.

Please download and install the following on any machines that utilize the TeamDynamix iPaaS Proxy.

  • Direct download: ASP.NET Core Runtime 10 Hosting Bundle
  • Direct download: .NET Desktop Runtime 10

What You Should Do Now

If your proxy is already on version 4.0 or higher: Install .NET 10 to ensure your proxy upgrades automatically when 4.1 is released this summer.

If your proxy is below version 4.0: First, confirm that at least .NET 8 is installed so the proxy can reach version 4.0. Then install .NET 10 to support the 4.1 upgrade. Alternatively, you may install .NET 10 now and force-upgrade your proxy to 4.1 on the release date.

notes.title: Easy to Configure
notes.bodyHtml:
Configuration of the Knowledge Base Article generation capability is simple. Simply open your desired Assist Agent Application on the AI & Automation layer. In the General tab of Agent Configuration, simply toggle on the switch labeled "Allow Article Generation." That's it!

Day 1 Impact - On day one of the release, a new switch will be present in the Assist Agent Application labeled Allow Article Generation.

notes.title: Generate Articles from Ticket Update Page
notes.bodyHtml:
Technicians may now generate Knowledge Base Articles from within the ticket update page. This ensures technicians can contribute to knowledge centered service (KCS) without heavily re-allocating time away from critical ticket work. Technicians need-only provide (on the ticket update page):

  • KB Article Category
  • KB Article Status
  • KB Article Template

Leveraging generative AI, a Knowledge Base Article will be created from the current ticket update page details, and the inputs above. Upon ticket update page save, the Knowledge Base Article will be created, attached to the ticket, and a comment denoting generation underway will be present on the ticket.

Knowledge Base side, the article will exist and once the asynchronous update of generated content is complete, the article will be updated & a feed entry denoting the update made by the Ticket Assist Agent will be present.

Day 1 Impact - Any newly created Assist Agent Applications on the AI & Automation layer will automatically include Article Generation.

notes.title: Product Nomenclature Updates
notes.bodyHtml:
Transition From "Bot" to "VSA"

As part of the Conversational AI 4.0 release, several key terms and component names have been updated to better reflect their functionality and align with industry-standard terminology. These changes are designed to make the platform more intuitive and easier to understand for both new and existing users. The table below outlines changes to naming of existing functionality:

Former Name Updated Name
Bot Operations & Logs VSA Operations & Logs
Bot Builder VSA Builder
Bot Choreographer VSA Choreographer
Bot Configuration VSA Configuration
Enterprise Bot Management Enterprise VSA Management
Intents Tasks
Entities Parameters
Shared Flows Sub Tasks
Bot Intent & Shared Flow Logs VSA Task & Sub Task Logs
Transfer to Bot Transfer to VSA
Live Chat Standard Messages Live Chat Message Templates
Day One Impact: On day one of Conversational AI 4.0, all navigation labels, documentation references, screen headers, and UI elements will reflect the new nomenclature. Users should familiarize themselves with the updated terminology, particularly the change from "Bot" to "VSA" and "Intents" to "Tasks," as these are the most frequently referenced terms. All existing functionality remains unchanged (unless otherwise noted in these release notes).

notes.title: Enhancements to API Management
notes.bodyHtml:
The 4.0 release includes (among other valuable enhancements) a significant overhaul to the API management capabilities of the platform. This includes automatically generated OpenAPI (swagger) documentation, query parameter support, call method configuration, and input data model manipulation. Along with this is also a total re-shaping of the layout (UI/UX) of API management related capabilities within applications. This section will drill into the specifics of each enhancement included in this release.

Query Parameter Support for Flow Endpoints

Prior to the release of version 4.0 flows when called via API endpoints could only accept input data within the request body. This upgrade allows for query parameter support for direct & indirect flow API calls enhancing the flexibility of API call structures made to run a flow.

NOTE: Only groups or properties in the input data model of a flow can accept query parameters.

Multiple REST Method Support

Within the new App API Management navigation section, there is a new sub-section for API Endpoints. In this section, the endpoints for all flows within the application can be viewed and HTTP method defined. For each flow within the application, one HTTP method may be defined, as well as an option to validate input data. This configuration impacts the API Management Documentation (section listed below) and how the flow's documentation will be presented.

API Management Documentation

With the additional configuration and control added to flow endpoints, we're now able to automatically generate OpenAPI / swagger formatted API documentation for flows within an iPaaS application. The documentation can be automatically hosted to a link provided by iPaaS, as well as users being able to download the OpenAPI generation from the application. For hosted documentation, users may also elect (via access group settings) which users, URLs, and IP range(s) can access the hosted documentation for maximum control.

Interface Reorganization

A new section on the left hand navigation for an iPaaS application is present. This section is labeled App API Management. Included in this section are the capabilities listed above, as well as some general re-organization of API Management adjacent capabilities for an application. Below is a list of existing configurations that have been moved to the App API Management section.

  • API Endpoint Security / Access Groups
  • Viewing Indirect Flow Endpoints
  • Viewing Direct Flow Endpoints

NOTE: To learn more about the API management capabilities of iPaaS, read our Using iPaaS' API Management Feature(s) KB article.
Day One Impact: On day one, a new section is added to the left hand navigation of iPaaS applications, App API Management. The following configurations have been moved to this new sub-section of the application: API Endpoint Security / Access Groups (under the new API Endpoint Security section), viewing Direct & Indirect Flow Endpoints (now under the API Endpoints section).

notes.title: Enhancements to Dynamic Forms
notes.bodyHtml:
New Form Element - "Grid"

A new, view only table element has been added to the form builder toolbox. This element is intended to be used for simply table presentation scenarios within a Dynamic Form. The element itself allows users to provide a JSON table (in a default static value, on condition, or on action) to be rendered in a Kendo UI table on the form.

NOTE: Before allowing the population of a JSON table into the form, users must define the columns within the element configuration settings that will be expected on the form.
Day One Impact: A new element is available in the formbuilder toolbox called grid.

Improved Conditions and Actions Management

Default Naming for Actions and Conditions

For new conditions & actions created / added to a form, an automatic title for the condition / action will be generated if none is provided. This allows form builders to save time & automatically create a syntactical naming convention for a form without ever giving a manual name to conditions or actions.

Day One Impact: When no title is provided for a condition or action being created, an automatic one will be generated dynamically based on the criteria being included on the condition / action setup.
Simplified Form Condition/Action Flow Maintenance

When adding a condition/action to a form element that utilizes a flow for the value source, a new button to create a flow for the value source has been added. This allows form builders the ability to quickly capture their idea for a condition/action, even if the flow to power that condition/action doesn't yet exist.

Additionally, for flows that power conditions / actions against a form there is an option to syncronize the data model of the flow, with the form via the "associated flows" section of the sidebar navigation.

Static HTML IDs Support

For form elements where CSS or JS might be interacting with an element on a form, prior to 4.0 there has been no way to set a static CSS ID. This means that as forms are updated / changes are made to them, the identifier for a form element may not remain static. Within the "appearance" tab of a form element configuration there is now an optional CSS Class value which can be used to define a static identifier for the form element, which CSS or JS may interact with.

Day One Impact: On day one, for the configuration view of a form element there will be a new option for CSS Class on the "appearance" tab.

Form Builder Agent Enhancements

The 4.0 release brings Form Builder Agents out of BETA. Added as part of the 3.4 release of iPaaS... the Dynamic Form Builder Agents allow users (prior to 4.0) to convert existing HTML forms to a dynamic form... create a Dynamic Form from a natural language description... or even make modifications to an existing Dynamic form from a natural language description.

NOTE: To learn more about the original BETA capabilities of Form Builder Agents, read our iPaaS 3.4 Release Notes KB article.
Create Forms from Images

Added for the full release of the Form Builder Agents suite, is the ability to convert an image of an existing form into a Dynamic Form. This is especially useful for forms that exist as "paper-copy-only" or physical forms that need migrated to become a digital form. Simply provide an image to the Form Builder Agent and the agent will take the image and parse it into a functional Dynamic Form in a matter of moments.

Speech-to-Text Form Creation

As an enhancement to the existing capability of a natural language description to create a new form, users may now use speech-to-text via the microphone icon to provide oral instructions to the agent to help with creating a form.

Day One Impact: On day one, a new option is available for the Form Agent Instructions to allow the input of an image file. Additionally, there is now a microphone icon which can accept speech to text to provide the agent with instruction.
NOTE: To learn more about the Form Builder Agents capability set, read our Working With Dynamic Form Builder Agents KB article.

notes.title: Enhanced Training Insights & Knowledge Management
notes.bodyHtml:
Suggested Content Additions

Added to the utterance analysis screen within a Conversational AI VSA (virtual service agent... formerly "Bot") application is a new tab labeled Suggested Tasks. This tab, using AI, aggregates requests from end users to the VSA that were not handled confidently, or at all via a task, knowledge set, or small talk. On this page, groupings of task suggestions are made based on topic similarity. This screen makes it much quicker for VSA application admins to identify what content to add to their VSA based on real world usage data.

NOTE: To learn more about utterance analysis or suggested tasks, please review our Reviewing and Resolving Utterances KB article.
Day One Impact: On day one of the Conversational AI 4.0 release, a new tab in utterance analysis called Suggested Tasks is viewable. This is also the new default view when opening the utterance analysis page.

Ticket Feed Ingestion for Knowledge Sets

For knowledge sets that contain a source type of TD Work Management Tickets, a new toggle is available to include or exclude the ticket feed from ingestion. For many organizations, the resolution details of a ticket may live in the feed content of a ticket. With this inclusion option, those potential resolution details can be ingested into a knowledge set for additional context when finding answers from a ticketing knowledge set source.

NOTE: To learn more about knowledge sets & the available source type(s), please review our Using The AI Knowledge Sets Feature KB article.

notes.title: Enhancements to Flows
notes.bodyHtml:
Increased Data Step Limitations

This release of iPaaS increases the data step limitation of flows significantly, from 20MB to 50MB. This is especially useful for flows that need to interact with large(r) data sets, or deal with APIs that do not allow for pagination naively.

NOTE: Though the data step limit is increased with this release, proper pagination & data cleansing is still recommended to ensure optimal flow runs for your organization.
NOTE: To learn more about the data step limit, read our About the AI & Automation Data Step Limit KB article.
Day One Impact: On day one, flows will be able to process 50MB of data from one step to another.

Flow Ownership Management

Owners/creators of flows may now transfer ownership of a flow within an application to another user with access to that application. This is especially helpful for situations where an original creator of a flow has left the organization, or has a change in role.

Day One Impact: In the Flow Configuration screen, next to the Owner detail, there is a button which allows the owner of a flow to transfer ownership.

Resource Substitution Improvements

Additional context has been added during resource substitution when an existing resource is not found or has a problem. Previously, the resource substitution dialog would simply show an "[Unknown]" tag next to the resource. This improves the context of what may be occurring or erroring during resource substitution.

Flow Failure Notification Behavior Change

Flow failure notifications will now only be sent when a flow truly fails during execution, not when it returns an HTTP status code greater than 300. Previously, any status code > 300 would trigger a failure notification, even though these codes don't necessarily indicate failures (e.g., 404 not found, 301 redirects). If your flow design requires failure notifications based on specific status codes or business logic, use the Stop with Error step to explicitly trigger notifications. This change only affects notification behavior... flow execution, status, and logs remain completely unchanged.

POTENTIALLY BREAKING CHANGE: If you have flows that return HTTP status codes greater than 300 as part of their normal operation and you currently rely on receiving failure notifications for these scenarios, you will need to update your flows to use the Stop with Error step where you want failure notifications to be triggered.
Day One Impact: On day one of the 4.0 release, flow failure notifications will only be sent for actual flow execution failures. Flows that return status codes > 300 but complete successfully will no longer trigger failure notifications unless explicitly configured to do so using the Stop with Error step.

notes.title: Live Chat Supervisor Enhancements
notes.bodyHtml:
Conversational Eavesdropping

Live agent supervisors for Conversational AI now have the ability to eavesdrop in on live chat conversations with or without notice to the live chat technician, but never with notice to the end user. This ensures that live chat supervisors have the opportunity to eavesdrop & ensure new live chat technicians are performing to expectations.

Conversational Takeover

Supervisors now also have the ability, when eavesdropping, to take over a live chat conversation from a live chat technician. This is useful when the end user requests escalation to a supervisor, or if a supervisor deems that a live chat technician is not de-escalating a chat well enough.

Pass Notes to Live Chat Technicians

Whilist eavesdropping, live chat supervisors may now pass notes to live chat technicians without notifying the end user. This is helpful for opportunities where a supervisor is hoping to give coaching or guidance to newer live chat technicians.

Take Live Chat Technicians Offline

In the live chat users screen, as a supervisor, you can now elect to take users offline. This is especially helpful to clear out technicians from the queue when a technician forgets to log off from live chat for lunch, a break, or even for the day.

NOTE: To learn more about the capabilities of live chat supervisors, please review our Using the Live Chat Supervisor Capabilities KB article.
Day One Impact: Supervisors can now monitor, coach, and intervene in live chat conversations in real-time. With the supervisor role, within live chat operations, supervisors may take over chats, sending private guidance to technicians mid-conversation, and remotely managing technician availability.

Simply provide an image to the Form Builder Agent and the agent will take the image and parse it into a functional Dynamic Form in a matter of moments.Speech-to-Text Form CreationAs an enhancement to the existing capability of a natural language description to create a new form, users may now use speech-to-text via the microphone icon to provide oral instructions to the agent to help with creating a form.NOTE: To learn more about the Form Builder Agents capability set, read our Working With Dynamic Form Builder Agents KB article.Day One Impact: On day one, a new option is available for the Form Agent Instructions to allow the input of an image file. Additionally, there is now a microphone icon which can accept speech to text to provide the agent with instruction.

notes.title: Administration & Performance Improvements
notes.bodyHtml:
Proxy Version Enforcement

To ensure full compatibility between proxy machine upgrades, on the back end we've implemented a proxy minimum version requirement. If proxies are below the known compatibility window version, the proxy will no longer operate without being updated. This upgrade is done to ensure that issues or problems do not arise from outdated, no longer supported versions of proxies experiencing backwards compatibility issues.

Day One Impact: On day one, all proxies will be updated to version 4.0 - For any proxies that do not successfully update to 4.0, TeamDynamix will be in touch with information to upgrade your proxy. After a period of time, proxies will be required to be on version 4.0 to continue functioning. Futher messaging & details will follow. For most, this will be entirely automatic.

Memory Usage Optimization

Several back end optimizations have been implemented to better manage memory, and cached data during flow processing. This helps improve flow performance with larger data sets, and also help to enable further data step limit size increases down the road.

Multiple SAML Certificate Support

For customers that may be migrating their SAML environment from one cert. to another, there may be two certs. utilized by the organization for a period of time during transition. To support this scenario, we have enabled users to provide multiple SAML certificates for their organization's SAML settings in iPaaS.

Day One Impact: On day one of iPaaS 4.0 - multiple SAML certificates may be provided in the organization settings under Security.

Custom Dashboard Toolbox Layout Updates

The dashboard builder has been redesigned with a domain-focused component organization. Components are now organized by entity type (Bots, Flows, Conversations, Assist Agent, etc.) rather than by visualization type (Charts, Tables, Widgets). This entity-first approach makes it easier to find and add components based on the data you want to display. Existing dashboards will continue to function without any changes required.

notes.title: User Management APIs
notes.bodyHtml:
Comprehensive User Management API Suite

We've introduced comprehensive User and User Group Management APIs that enable programmatic control over your user base. These APIs provide full CRUD operations for both users and user groups, along with specialized endpoints for managing user status (active/inactive), group memberships, and role assignments. This allows administrators to automate user provisioning, deprovisioning, and permission management through API calls rather than manual configuration.

notes.title: Enhancements to Tasks & Sub Tasks
notes.bodyHtml:
Standard "Show Ticket" Step

A new option has been added to the "Show" step for rendering a TeamDynamix ticket. This option allows for the input of a credential, application ID, and ticket ID to form the HTML presentation of a ticket to the end user. This makes it so that users do not need to create custom cards, or custom HTML to present a ticket as part of a task.

NOTE: To learn more about the available options for showing content, please review our Using The Task Toolbox: Conversation Section KB article.
Day One Impact: When viewing the edit configuration for a show step, a new option for content type "TeamDynamix Ticket" is available in the dropdown.

Updated No-Response Response Messaging

New VSAs created after the release of 4.0 will now have default "no-match" messaging of: I'm sorry. I am unable to respond based on the information I have at my disposal. Please try rephrasing your request.

Increased Data Step Limitations

This release of CAI increases the data step limitation of flows significantly, from 20MB to 50MB. This is especially useful for flows that need to interact with large(r) data sets, or deal with APIs that do not allow for pagination naively.

NOTE: Though the data step limit is increased with this release, proper pagination & data cleansing is still recommended to ensure optimal flow runs for your organization.
NOTE: To learn more about the data step limit, read our About the AI & Automation Data Step Limit KB article.
Day One Impact:On day one, flows will be able to process 50MB of data from one step to another.

Task Ownership Management

Owners/creators of sub-tasks may now transfer ownership of a sub-task within an application to another user with access to that application. This is especially helpful for situations where an original creator of a sub-task has left the organization, or has a change in role.

Day One Impact: In the Flow Configuration screen, next to the Owner detail, there is a button which allows the owner of a flow to transfer ownership.

Resource Substitution Improvements

Additional context has been added during resource substitution when an existing resource is not found or has a problem. Previously, the resource substitution dialog would simply show an "[Unknown]" tag next to the resource. This improves the context of what may be occurring or erroring during resource substitution.

Task Failure Notification Behavior Change

Task failure notifications will now only be sent when a task truly fails during execution, not when it returns an HTTP status code greater than 300. Previously, any status code > 300 would trigger a failure notification, even though these codes don't necessarily indicate failures (e.g., 404 not found, 301 redirects). If your task design requires failure notifications based on specific status codes or business logic, use the Stop with Error step to explicitly trigger notifications. This change only affects notification behavior... task execution, status, and logs remain completely unchanged.

POTENTIALLY BREAKING CHANGE: If you have tasks that return HTTP status codes greater than 300 as part of their normal operation and you currently rely on receiving failure notifications for these scenarios, you will need to update your tasks to use the Stop with Error step where you want failure notifications to be triggered.
Day One Impact: On day one of the 4.0 release, task failure notifications will only be sent for actual task execution failures. Tasks that return status codes > 300 but complete successfully will no longer trigger failure notifications unless explicitly configured to do so using the Stop with Error step.

notes.title: Dashboard & Reporting Enhancements
notes.bodyHtml:
New Knowledge Set Metrics Custom Dashboard Elements

A handful of new dashboard elements have been introduced with 4.0 specifically around knowledge sets. Below is a list & description of new dashboard elements added:

  • Knowledge Set Usage By Period Chart: Allows dashboard users to view successes and failures over a selectable period of time for all knowledge sets. The time selection options are: daily this month, hourly today, daily last seven days, monthly this year, monthly last year, hourly yesterday and daily last month.
  • Knowledge Set Usage By Period Chart - Specific Knowledge Set: Allows dashboard users to view successes and failures over a selectable period of time for a specfic knowledge set. The time selection options are: daily this month, hourly today, daily last seven days, monthly this year, monthly last year, hourly yesterday and daily last month.
  • Knowledge Set Usage Chart: Allows dashboard users to view successes and failures over a selectable period of time for all knowledge sets. Time time selection options are: yesterday, last 7 days, this month and last month.
  • Knowledge Set Usage Chart - Specific Knowledge Set: Allows dashboard users to view successes and failures over a selectable period of time for a specific knowledge set. Time time selection options are: yesterday, last 7 days, this month and last month.

VSA Response Training Report Report Updates

The VSA Response Training report has been updated to be significantly more useful. Previously this report would only provide a table of information with the following columns: VSA Name, Deployed Version, Utterance, Matched Task, Matched Confidence, Created Time, ApplicationID, and Application Name.

The updated report includes the following detail, giving VSA administrators access to far more information with respect to VSA training reports: ApplicationID, Application Name, ChatBot Name, Message Created Time, Deployed Version, User's Utterance, Handled (yes or no), Handled by CLU Task, Handled by LLM Task, Handled by Small Talk, Handled by Knowledge Set, Matched Task, Match Confidence, Utterance Type, No Task Match Fount, Multiple Tasks Matched, Knowledge Set Name, Quantity of Knowledge Set Matches, and Small Talk Response.

Day One Impact: Existing data pulls reliant on the VSA Reponse Training report will need to be re-worked as the data structure for this report is significantly changed.

Custom Dashboard Toolbox Layout Updates

The dashboard builder has been redesigned with a domain-focused component organization. Components are now organized by entity type (VSAs, Flows, Conversations, Assist Agent, etc.) rather than by visualization type (Charts, Tables, Widgets). This entity-first approach makes it easier to find and add components based on the data you want to display. Existing dashboards will continue to function without any changes required.

notes.title: Private Attachments
notes.bodyHtml:
Like Feed entries, Attachments can now be marked as private, which hides the attachment from users in the client portal. When viewing a ticket, technicians can toggle whether an attachment is visible in the client portal using the Private checkbox.

A new ticketing application setting "Make attachments private by default" allows controlling the default setting for attachments uploaded by technicians. When a new attachment is added, it will be marked as private or not based on these rules:

  • Attachments uploaded via client portal default to not private.
  • Attachments uploaded via Work Management or via email are set based on the application setting. Users cannot change this setting during upload of an attachment; they will need to change it on the existing attachment.
  • Attachments added via the API are set based on the application setting, but a new parameter allows setting it to either private or not.
  • Attachments copied via the API use the same value as the copied attachment has, but a new parameter allows setting it to either private or not.
Day 1 Impact - The ability to mark attachments as private will be available to all ticketing users immediately.

notes.title: Permissions to delete attachments on tickets
notes.bodyHtml:
New permissions have been added for both technicians and client users to determine if they can delete attachments on tickets. The "Delete Ticket Attachments via Client Portal" permission on a client security role allows client users to delete attachments, and the "Delete Attachments" permission on the ticketing application security role allows users to delete attachments via the technician interface.

These permissions have been automatically assigned to all security roles to avoid a change in users' permissions.

notes.title: Filtering on Dashboards for Report Builder
notes.bodyHtml:
Report Builder reports on dashboards can be filtered within the dashboard based on the fields that are configured as filterable. The "Prompt" column in report builder filters has been renamed to "Dashboard Filtering" in support of this feature.

Day 1 Impact - The filters defined on reports will be immediately available in dashboards.

notes.title: Start a Teams Chat from TeamDynamix
notes.bodyHtml:

  • From a ticket, use the Actions > Start Teams Chat menu to start a conversation including people on the current ticket.
  • From the TeamDynamix mobile app, use the Person Detail page to start a direct chat or call via Teams.

[Screenshot of starting a chat via mobile COMING SOON]

To learn more about the Start Teams Chat feature, see the following articles:

  • Installing and Configuring the Microsoft Teams Integration
  • Using TeamDynamix with Microsoft Teams - overview of all end-user features in the Teams integration
  • Starting a Teams Chat from TeamDynamix - overview of the new Teams features in version 12.1
This feature will not be available in the Release Preview environment. It relies on the Teams integration's connection to your TeamDynamix production environment and relies on a new version of the mobile app which will be released alongside version 12.1.

notes.title: AI Ticket Summarization
notes.bodyHtml:
AI Ticket Summaries allow technicians to quickly understand the details and recent activity on a ticket.

To learn more about AI Ticket Summaries, see this article.

This feature was previously released in a maintenance release.

Real-Time calculation of Ticket Metrics

Ticket Metrics in the report builder are now calculated as tickets are modified, rather than as a batch process overnight. This allows reports that rely on these metrics to be more up to date and means that historic tickets' values are no longer modified unless the ticket is modified.

Day 1 Impact - This change may cause differences in reporting on these metrics for historic tickets when Operational Hours are changed in the organization. Prior to version 12.1, a change in the Operational Hours would change the relevant metrics for all tickets in the ticketing application, even if that ticket had not been changed recently. Starting in version 12.1, metrics for old tickets will not be recalculated in that scenario.

notes.title: Reassign Issues on Update Issue page
notes.bodyHtml:
Users can reassign issues on the Update Issue page, and can notify the assigned resources. ​​​

Note that the Notify Responsible User does not check whether the responsible user for the issue has changed, meaning it works the same way as the Issue Edit page, not the Update Ticket page.

Day 1 Impact - The ability to reassign issues from the Update page is available to all users immediately.

notes.title: Shared Project Template Editing
notes.bodyHtml:
New permissions allow users to collaborate on project templates that they did not create.

  • Edit Global Project Templates - the user can edit templates that are marked as global, even if they are not the template owner.
  • View All Project Templates - the user can view project templates in Work Management that are not marked as global
  • Edit All Project Templates - the user can edit all project templates in Work Management, including ones that are not marked as global.

notes.title: People Import Security Role Update
notes.bodyHtml:
The people import will be updated to better handle users where the security role does not match the applications assigned. When the people import processes a user that is assigned to a security role with a Client or Client + Reporting license type, the user's access to the TDNext application will be removed.

Day 1 Impact - If there are people in your people import who are assigned to the TDNext application and have a client license type, their access to the TDNext application will be automatically removed. If this inadvertently removes TDNext access from a user, you can address this by assigning a non-client license type and re-assigning the TDNext application (either through a new import or by manually updating the user).

notes.title: Automation Rules for modified tickets
notes.bodyHtml:
Automation Rules can now be configured to apply to tickets after ticket creation. Existing rules will only apply during ticket creation until edited.

When automation rules apply to existing tickets, new settings allow setting values without overriding configured values.

To learn more about Automation Rules, see the updated Getting Started with Automation Rules article.

notes.title: Update Ticket workflow step type
notes.bodyHtml:
The new Update Ticket workflow step type allows a ticket workflow to make changes directly on the ticket without needing any external automation. Administrators can configure the workflow to make the following changes:

  • Change Status and related settings
  • Set Responsibility
  • Set custom attributes
  • Add a comment (and optionally send a notification).

To learn more about the Update Ticket step type, see this article.

notes.bodyHtml:

notes.title: Improved Integration with TeamDynamix ITAM (Sassafras)
notes.bodyHtml:
The integration between TeamDynamix Work Management and TeamDynamix IT Asset Management (Sassafras AllSight) has been updated with the following improvements:

  • Data about computers, devices and software products that is synced from ITAM into Work Management is stored in dedicated fields that are available for reporting and visible from the Discovered Data tab of the detail page.
  • The integration no longer relies on the External ID field, but uses dedicated fields to link Work Management assets and CIs to ITAM computers, devices and software products.
  • Setup within Work Management is reduced. You are no longer required to set up external viewers or custom attributes for discovered data.
  • The integration runs faster as it makes more changes in bulk.

To learn more about the integration to TeamDynamix ITAM see these articles:

  • [Articles COMING SOON]
The updated integration will require Work Management version 12.1 or later and ITAM version [TBD - this is expected to be a specific maintenance release of ITAM version 8.1] or later.

notes.title: Intune Actions in Work Management
notes.bodyHtml:
The Microsoft Intune integration has been updated to support taking actions via Intune directly within TeamDynamix. The following actions are now supported:

  • Open in Intune
  • Sync TeamDynamix Asset from Intune
  • Remotely Lock Device
  • Device Reboot
  • Reset Device Passcode
  • Run Intune Device Sync
  • Windows Defender Full Scan
  • Windows Defender Quick Scan
  • Initiate Windows Defender Updates
  • Request Remote Assistance

To learn more about the Intune integration, see this article.
